summary: Decoy npm package exfiltrates developer identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint on install
details: ai-sdk-helpers@1.0.0 advertises Vercel AI SDK helper functionality but ships no implementation — package.json declares main as dist/index.js, but no dist/ directory exists in the tarball. The package's only effective behavior is in scripts/postinstall.js, which runs automatically on `npm install`. That script reads installer-side identity files including ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, the consumer repository's.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to harvest the developer's SCM email and GitHub login. It additionally collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo() (OS username), os.platform(), process.cwd(), CI-provider indicators, and identity-shaped environment variables (GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, EMAIL, etc.). The harvested JSON is POSTed to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. The README's claim of fully anonymous telemetry is contradicted by the payload, which carries raw email addresses, GitHub usernames, and hostnames rather than hashes. The hollow library shape combined with install-time identity harvest is a textbook supply-chain reconnaissance / decoy package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
